content: The linguistic double meaning of 'reflexion' (as both a technical term for autonomous AI self-reflection and a high school English vocabulary term for metacognitive reflection) reveals that LLM self-reflection mechanisms are essentially formalizing the same reflective learning process that human K-12 education explicitly teaches. We can hypothesize that grading rubrics designed for teaching reflective essay writing in high school English can be repurposed to fine-tune and evaluate the quality of LLM self-reflection.
